math_definition: RReLU(x) = x if x >= 0, a*x if x < 0; training: a ~ Uniform(lower, upper); eval: a = (lower + upper) / 2

approach: Two separate functions: calculate_rrelu_eval (SFPI, like prelu) and calculate_rrelu_training (TTI, like rand+leaky_relu). Precompute range=upper-lower on host. Training uses PRNG for per-element random slope.
New LLK dispatch file with init (seeds PRNG with seed=0) and 3-param dispatch to calculate_rrelu
Follows selu pattern - calls llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_rrelu directly with 3 params (lower, range, training)
Added SFPU_OP_RRELU_INCLUDE guard block after PRELU block
Added rrelu entry to SfpuType enum in both architectures
Added RRELU entry to UnaryOpType enum
Added RRELU to macro definition (SFPU_OP_RRELU_INCLUDE) and parameterized init/func with 3 params. Precomputes range=upper-lower before passing to kernel. get_op_approx_mode uses default false.
Added RRELU to is_parametrized_type switch (3 params: lower, upper, training)
Custom function declaration (not macro) with 3 params: lower=0.125, upper=1/3, training=false. Converts training bool to float flag before passing to UnaryWithParam.
Custom bind_rrelu function with 3 keyword params: lower=0.125, upper=1/3, training=false. Bound directly to ttnn::rrelu.
Added _golden_function_rrelu using torch.nn.functional.rrelu with lower, upper, training kwargs. Attached to ttnn.rrelu.
Full build_metal.sh succeeded with zero errors. All 12 layers compile cleanly.
